---
title: "Recipe: automatic post-commit code/prose review (codex, Claude fallback)"
---

A worked [hooks](hooks.qmd) example: after **every git commit**, a read-only reviewer looks at that
one commit (HEAD vs its parent) and returns findings to both you and Claude. The primary reviewer is
`codex`; if codex is rate-limited it falls back to a headless `claude` review of the same diff. It
is **advisory only**: Claude is told not to auto-fix, but to assess, present, and propose a plan you
approve.

- Wiring: `~/.claude/settings.json` (`hooks.PostToolUse`, matcher `Bash`).
- Script: `~/.claude/hooks/codex-commit-review.sh` (+ a companion `*.README.md`).

## Wiring

```jsonc
// ~/.claude/settings.json
"hooks": {
  "PostToolUse": [
    { "matcher": "Bash",
      "hooks": [
        { "type": "command",
          "command": "bash \"$HOME/.claude/hooks/codex-commit-review.sh\"",
          "timeout": 600,
          "statusMessage": "codex reviewing the commit..." } ] } ]
}
```

The hook runs after every `Bash` tool call; the script decides whether to act.

## When it fires

1. The command string contains `git commit` (so `git add ... && git commit ...` and `--amend` both
   qualify; `--dry-run`/`--help` skipped).
2. HEAD is **< 30 seconds old**, confirming a commit was actually created (a failed/no-op commit
   leaves HEAD older, so nothing fires).

It also needs `jq`, `codex` on PATH (the script prepends the nvm node bin), and a `claude` binary for
the fallback. It **always exits 0**: a review can never break the turn.

## The review (codex, read-only)

```bash
codex exec --sandbox read-only -c approval_policy=never --skip-git-repo-check -o <file> "$PROMPT"
```

Read-only sandbox (cannot mutate the repo) and non-interactive (cannot hang). One retry with
`danger-full-access` only if codex's `bwrap` network namespace fails (`RTM_NEWADDR`), safe because
the prompt is strictly read-only.

The prompt: scope to HEAD-vs-parent (`git show HEAD`, changed hunks only); prioritize manuscripts,
slide decks, R, C/C++, and plan files; flag correctness regressions (code) and accuracy/consistency/
convention drift (prose); learn project rules from `CLAUDE.md`/`AGENTS.md`/`README`/`docs/`; output
concise `- [severity] title -- path:line` lines.

## Claude fallback

If codex is empty or matches `usage limit|rate limit|quota|try again at|429|ERROR:|stream error`, the
script re-reviews the same `git show HEAD` diff (capped ~200k chars) with
`claude -p ... --model claude-sonnet-4-6 --permission-mode plan` (no tools). The label becomes
`claude (codex fallback)` and a caveat is attached: treat it as a **same-model-family mechanical
safety net**, not an independent review, and re-run codex later. Override the model with
`CLAUDE_REVIEW_MODEL`.

## What you and Claude receive

One JSON object: `systemMessage` shows you the banner + review; `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext`
feeds Claude the **protocol note**: assess each finding, discard false positives, present the genuine
ones, propose a fix plan, then **wait for your decision** (no auto-editing).

## Good to know

- **Amends re-trigger the review.** Each `--amend` is a new HEAD < 30s old, so iterating on one
  commit spawns a fresh review each time.
- Treat fallback findings as a net, not gospel: in practice it both catches real bugs and recycles
  occasional false positives. Verify before acting.
- The script header comment says `timeout: 300` while settings sets `600`; settings wins.

## Tuning

- `export CLAUDE_REVIEW_MODEL=claude-opus-4-8` for a stronger (still non-independent) fallback.
- Edit `REVIEW_PROMPT` in the script to change priority file types or severity guidance.
- Disable: comment out the `PostToolUse`/`Bash` block in `~/.claude/settings.json`.
